steps.title: Identify the evidence-claim relationship
steps.content: The linguist assumes “know” appears quite often, then the passage provides corpus data: “know” is the 8th most commonly used verb. This data supports the assumption rather than contradicting it.

steps.title: Match to the correct word
steps.content: Substantiate means to support or verify with proof or evidence—this is exactly what the corpus data does to the linguist's assumption. Refute, entail, and transpose mean to disprove, to logically require, and to rearrange respectively, none fitting the relationship where evidence backs a claim. That matches choice A — A is correct.
